title: Market Brief — Sunday, May 17, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while B200s at 25% signal scarcity and GB200s at 10% availability mark a severe shortage with no transparent pricing. The biggest price signal is B200 spot at $4.20/hr median with a $2 spread, reflecting acute demand for next-gen training silicon.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while B200s at 25% signal scarcity and GB200s at 10% availability mark a severe shortage with no transparent pricing. The biggest price signal is B200 spot at $4.20/hr median with a $2 spread, reflecting acute demand for next-gen training silicon.

At 1.25x TCO-adjusted costs, Llama 70B inference runs roughly $9.48/M on A100, $9.84/M on H100, and $11.90/M on MI300X. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling far below production cost, burning cash at an estimated loss of $9-11/M tokens depending on hardware. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output prints premium margins. Provider grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 and GB200 compute is undervalued given scarcity at 25% and 10% availability respectively, and locking in B200 spot below $4.00/hr is the cleanest trade on the board. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued relative to GPT-5.4 at $15/M, which benchmarks competitively, making o3 Pro demand unsustainable at 5x the spread.
bullets: 85% A100 availability signals oversupply in legacy compute.
10% GB200 availability is a severe shortage with no listed spot price.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss of roughly $9.10/M against TCO-adjusted A100 inference.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$4.20/hr B200 spot median carries a 57% spread between low and high, signaling illiquid price discovery.
25% B200 availability puts next-gen training compute squarely in the scarce zone.
